concept: Wondrous / Wonderful / Exceeding What Was Looked For
description: Wondrous: exceeding what was looked for, and lifting the looker as it passes. A summer that outgrows every plan for it, a change no story prepared, a morning that makes the sleeper sit up. Where 'phelora' (beautiful) names what satisfies the eye and 'horae' (radiant) what shines outward, 'waolia' names what overflows the expectation itself: the quality in things that makes a person say 'wao'.
sound_symbolism: The word is 'wao' (wow) taught to stand still and describe: the same caught-open breath, settled into an adjective. Nearly all vowel, it holds the mouth open the whole way through, 'wa.o' wide, 'li' lifting, 'a' refusing to close; wonder does not purse its lips.
grammatical_notes: Adjective. 'ha keloi waolia to nai.' (That summer was wondrous.) 'whekelua ru kuenora nela ru waolia nai.' (Magic is very strange and very wondrous.) Kin to 'wao' (wow), the response it earns, and 'wakomi' (surprise), the arrival it often rides in on. The quality noun, wonder, comes by rule.

pillars.secular-buddhist-philosophy: Wonder is attention caught fully awake; a word for it keeps the practice of being astonishable.
pillars.art-nouveau-aesthetics: The movement built whole doorways to say this word: the ordinary vine, drawn until it exceeds itself.

tags.cognition: What outruns what was looked for
